data.title: Glassnode, Swissblock, and Willy Woo collaborate on Bitcoin Vector #35 for detailed analysis on Bitcoin, Ethereum, and DeFi.
data.description: The latest Bitcoin Vector #35, a collaboration between Glassnode, Swissblock, and Willy Woo, provides an in-depth market analysis focusing on Bitcoin, Ethereum, and DeFi. The report offers detailed insights and data on the current trends and developments in the crypto market. It presents a comprehensive overview of key metrics and indicators for investors and enthusiasts to stay informed. Find out more about the analysis in the full report.

data.title: Advantest wins Excellent Performance Award at TSMC Forum for Excellent Production Support, first time recipient.
data.description: On December 24, 2025, Advantest Corporation, a top supplier of semiconductor test equipment listed as TSE: 6857, revealed their win of TSMC's Excellent Performance Award for Excellent Production Support. This marks Advantest's inaugural award at TSMC's annual Supply Chain Management Forum, signifying their industry recognition.

data.title: Li-FT Power acquires 50% interest in Galinée Property from Azimut, signing definitive project agreement.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia on December 24, 2025, Li-FT Power Ltd. ("Li-FT") has announced that it has finalized a project acquisition agreement with Azimut Exploration Inc. ("Azimut"). This agreement involves Li-FT acquiring 50% of the exclusive exploration rights for the Galinée property, known as the Galinée Transaction. This follows Li-FT's recent press release on December 14, 2025, regarding its scheme implementation deed with Winsome Resources Ltd. and a non-binding letter of intent with Azimut and SOQUEM Inc. The transaction is subject to meeting specific conditions. Li-FT is listed on the TSXV under LIFT, OTCQX as LIFFF, and Frankfurt as WS0, while Azimut is listed on the TSXV under AZM and OTCQX as AZMTF.

data.title: Bybit's 29th Proof-of-Reserves report released. Second-largest crypto exchange. Verified data as of Dec. 17, 2025.
data.description: In Dubai, UAE on December 24, 2025, Bybit, the world's second-largest cryptocurrency exchange based on trading volume, released its 29th Proof-of-Reserves (PoR) snapshot showcasing data as of December 17, 2025. The publication was verified independently by Hacken.

data.title: Sen. Warren warns against Trump's Fed nominee, saying President's loyalty over monetary independence could lead to a 'puppet.'
data.description: Senator Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) is expressing concern over President Donald Trump's remarks regarding his selection for the next Chair of the Federal Reserve once Jerome Powell steps down in May 2026. Warren criticized Trump's statement on Truth Social, where he indicated that anyone who opposes him will not be appointed as the Fed Chairman. She believes this remark signifies Trump's intention to prioritize loyalty over the independence of the Federal Reserve, suggesting he plans to nominate a 'puppet' who will cater to his interests rather than those of the American people. Warren emphasized the gravity of Trump's words, warning that they will taint any nominee put forth by him. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.description: A price prediction for WLD suggests a medium-term recovery to $0.67 if the support at $0.47 is maintained. However, bearish momentum indicates a possible decline to a downside target of $0.30.

data.title: Federal judge dismisses Chamber of Commerce's lawsuit over Trump's $100,000 H-1B visa fee, citing president's authority.
data.description: A federal judge in Washington, D.C., has rejected the U.S. Chamber of Commerce's challenge to the $100,000 fee imposed by President Donald Trump on new H-1B visas for highly skilled foreign workers. U.S. District Judge Beryl Howell dismissed the challenge stating that the fee falls under the president's authority to regulate immigration. The Chamber of Commerce argued that the fee goes against federal immigration law and would result in job cuts and reduced services by many companies, hospitals, and other employers. However, Judge Howell, appointed by former Democratic President Barack Obama, dismissed these claims. This decision was made public on Benzinga.com, where further details can be found.
